key_points: Random freshman roommate assignment with no choice allowed
Gender-inclusive housing available and appreciated by queer students
Students anxious about roommate compatibility before arrival
key_quotes: I've been having nightmares about who my roommate could be. What if they are a psychopath?
I am planning to choose gender inclusive housing as a priority because I socially feel most welcomed around queer/diverse community
Anyone know how close the closest non-Dartmouth parking space is and its safety/pricing?

key_points: Greek life and drinking culture dominate the social scene heavily
Non-drinkers report feeling anxious about fitting in, but options exist
Tight-knit community is real; people bond deeply across outdoor activities
key_quotes: I don't know how to explain it, but Dartmouth definitely is the frat college of the Ivy League. It just has that vibe.
A small minority is very vocal, but most of the college does not reflect their lifestyle.
if you're not the sort of person who loves partying or drinking, do you feel at all excluded on campus?

key_points: Meal plan system is confusing; swipes vs dining dollars unclear
FoCo (main dining) has unlimited swipe-in access once entered
Collis and specialty dining spots add variety but rules are opaque
key_quotes: I still don't understand our meal plan system
The thing I'm having trouble getting clear answers on is which locations you have to spend your dining dollars at
Mainly interested in if you can use meal swipes for Collis, since I saw they serve sushi

key_points: Quarter system's intensity means illness forces full medical leave
Missing even one week of class can guarantee a failing grade
Academic pressure combined with isolation creates real mental health risk
key_quotes: Due to the short length and high stress of each term, becoming seriously mentally or physically ill means that you basically have to drop everything and go home on a medical leave
missing as much as a week in some classes can guarantee a C
all the prestige and academic pressure crushed my mental health

key_points: Full-ride packages exist and are genuinely transformative for low-income students
International students face complicated aid issues; inherited assets counted as liquid
Financial aid appeals are possible but stressful and slow to resolve
key_quotes: Dartmouth is treating my parents' inherited retirement home as a major asset even though it is not liquid or realistically sellable
I got in to Colorado — DARTMOUTH WITH A FULL RIDE WOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO
I could never go to Dartmouth at $70,000 a year though

key_points: Quarter system is intense; courses move fast and punish any absence
Small class sizes and professor access are genuine strengths
D-Plan flexibility surprises students once they actually live it
key_quotes: What's something about the D-Plan that you didn't understand until you actually lived it?
The reality of how it shapes your social life, your friendships, and your entire Dartmouth experience is something you can't grasp until you're in it
Dartmouth has a great Engineering program... extremely loyal alumni, who help each other for life

key_points: President's response to Trump demands was vague and non-committal
Financial aid disputes with administration can be slow and unresolved
Portal astrology culture reflects lack of transparency in admissions communication
key_quotes: She says a lot about being fiercely committed to academic freedom, but doesn't actually say whether or not they reject the compact. Kinda seems like a non-response.
I am worried we may not resolve the financial aid issues before winter break
There is so much portal astrology in this sub, and it's all total bullshit

key_points: Hanover is genuinely rural; car access dramatically changes your experience
Outdoor culture — skiing, hiking, nature — is core to Dartmouth identity
Getting out of town without a car is legitimately difficult
key_quotes: It's a bubble. When I say it's rural, I mean it's rural. There is a 10-mile radius of stuff to do, but after you get out of that, unless you're a big skier or outdoorsy person, there's not a lot.
There's nothing to do within 100 miles culture in this region. Where are all the buildings?!
I work at The D for 14 years… you're on point with this and yes, there is NOTHING to do here

key_points: Consulting and finance dominate post-grad culture; students feel pressure to conform
Alumni network is genuinely close-knit and valuable across industries
Strong pipeline to IB and consulting but non-finance paths feel underserved
key_quotes: why does everybody want to go into consulting... I feel like I'm wasting the 'dartmouth' opportunity or a failure bc I dont want to
I want to go into IB or consulting after graduation. I've really romanticized Dartmouth
Is dartmouth's alumni network and community really THAT close-knit?

key_points: Full financial aid makes it free for low-income students — a genuine deal
Without aid, cost approaches $90k/year which shocks even committed families
International students face the worst deal: full cost, minimal aid access
key_quotes: Dartmouth offered me essentially a full-ride financial aid package
my parents were overjoyed that I got into an Ivy League and quickly agreed to pay the full tuition + other expenses associated, which they knew was going to be ~90k
Full ride at Dartmouth vs paying full at Stanford as an international — worth the debt?

key_points: Outdoor-obsessed, tight-knit, and proudly weird — not a typical Ivy
Students either love the isolation or find it quietly suffocating
Greek life sets the social tone whether you participate or not
key_quotes: I love the balance between nature, party culture, and academia and every single person I met either reminded me of myself or was simply the nicest and/or coolest human
I'm a very much not that kind of person. I don't drink or smoke... Naturally, I was very concerned about making friends
Dartmouth is definitely the frat college of the Ivy League. It just has that vibe.

key_points: **Mental Health:** Medical leave is the only option if you get seriously ill mid-term
**Drinking Culture:** Multiple local workers confirm pervasive alcohol culture on campus
**Isolation:** No train station; getting off-campus without a car is genuinely hard
key_quotes: The smell of empty beer cans wafting over the whole place — I worked on campus for 17 years
God help you if you get sick mid-term and can't get extensions — you basically have to drop out temporarily
why is it so hard to get out of Hanover? There isn't even a train station

key_points: First Year Trips bond students before classes start — genuinely beloved
Alumni network activates fast and feels personal, not transactional
D-Plan off-terms let students do internships and travel in unique ways
key_quotes: Everyone from my former professors, to the staff and students have become family to me. 9+ years later, we are all one big, international family.
The D-Plan flexibility... the reality of how it shapes your social life is something you can't grasp until you're in it
Be a drunk or Olympic athlete; a basket weaver or physicist — it's there
